title: Hong Kong Cat Vaccination Requirements
summary: Hong Kong regulates cat imports through a tiered group system based on rabies status. Cats must be microchipped, vaccinated against rabies (for non-Group I), feline panleukopenia, and feline respiratory disease complex. Quarantine requirements vary from 0-120 days depending on origin country group.

group_i.name: Rabies-Free Countries
group_i.notes: Normally exempt from quarantine if all permit conditions met

group_ii.name: Rabies-Controlled Countries
group_ii.notes: No quarantine if compliant; rabies vaccination 30 days to 12 months before entry

group_iiia.name: Rabies-Infected Countries (NEW)
group_iiia.notes: Mandatory 30-day quarantine; rabies antibody titer test required

group_iiib.name: Other Countries
group_iiib.countries: All other countries not in Group I, II, or IIIA
group_iiib.notes: Mandatory 120-day quarantine; rabies antibody titer test required

rabies.timing: Between 30 days and 12 months before entry

feline_panleukopenia.name: Feline Panleukopenia (FPV) / Infectious Enteritis
feline_panleukopenia.notes: Also known as feline distemper; core vaccine for all cats

microchip.notes: Must be readable by universal scanner

import_permit.application_lead_time: At least 3 working days (5 days from receipt)

health_certificate.endorsement_required: Yes, by governing authority in origin country

resident_cats_in_hk.dog_licensing: Not applicable to cats (dogs >5 months require license + rabies vaccination)
resident_cats_in_hk.notes: No mandatory vaccination requirement for resident cats in HK under AFCD regulations; however, vet-recommended core vaccines include FVRCP and FeLV

disability_assistance_cats.status: Not explicitly covered in AFCD policy (DAD refers to dogs only)

bengal_cats.notes: Must contact AFCD for required documents

key_contacts.address: Permit & Certification Section, Import & Export Division, 5th Floor, Agriculture, Fisheries and Conservation Department, Cheung Sha Wan Government Offices, 303 Cheung Sha Wan Road, Kowloon, Hong Kong
